uk: 🕃 Преподобного отця нашого Спиридона, чудотворця, єпископа Трімитійського.
saints.en: Ven. Spyridon the Wonderworker, Bishop of Trimythous

rubrics:
Вечірня: катизма чергова. На «Господи, взиваю» - 6 стихир прп., Сл., І нині: богородичний-догмат 3-го гласу. Прокімен дня. Стиховня Октоїха. По «Отче наш»: тропар прп., Сл., І нині: богород. воскресний за гласом тропаря прп. Відпуст великий з помин. цілого тижня і прп.
Утреня: по «Бог Господь»: тропар прп. (2), Сл., І нині: богород. воскресний за гласом тропаря прп. Катизми чергові, сідальні Октоїха. Пс. 50. Канон: якщо Господній або Богородичний храм, то – канон храму, прп. та Октоїха. Якщо храм святого, то канон прп., храмового святого та Октоїха. По 9-ій пісні – світильний Октоїха, Сл.: прп., І нині: його богород. Стиховня Октоїха. По «Отче наш»: тропар прп., Сл., І нині: богород. відпустовий за гласом тропаря прп. і за днем тижня. Відпуст великий з помин. усього тижня і прп.
Часи: на 1-му – тропар і кондак дня; на 6-му – храму; на 3-му та 9-му – прп.
Літургія: вся Служба – дня. Ап. – Еф. 220 зач.; 2, 11-13. Єв. – Лк. 72 зач.; 13, 18-29.

troparion_en.text: At the First Council you appeared as a champion of Truth and a Wonderworker, O our God-bearing Father Spyridon.  / For you spoke to one dead in her grave, and transformed a serpent into gold;  / while chanting your holy prayers /  you had Angels serving with you, most holy one.  / Glory to Him Who glorified you.  / Glory to Him Who crowned you.  / Glory to Him Who, through you, grants healing to all.

kontakion_en.text: Wounded by the love of Christ, most holy one, your mind was given wings by the radiance of the Spirit, O God-inspired one,  / you found your work in active contemplation, O God-pleasing Spyridon,  / becoming a godly sacrifice, and entreating divine illumination for all.

matched_oca_saint: Saint Spyridon the Wonderworker, Bishop of Tremithus

troparion.text: The truth of things revealed you to your flock as a rule of faith,  / a model of meekness, and a teacher of temperance.  /  Therefore, through humility, you attained the heights;  / and through poverty, riches.  /  O Hierarch Mardarije* our Father, entreat Christ God that our souls may be saved.

kontakion.text: Tireless preacher of Christ the Lord, leader on the path of Saint Savva for your people in diaspora,  / labor-loving practitioner and teacher of repentance,  / Saint Mardarije* of Libertyville and America, together with all the Enlighteners of the American lands,  / entreat the only Lover of mankind, to grant  peace and unity to all Orthodox Christians.

oca_saints.name: Saint Spyridon the Wonderworker, Bishop of Tremithus

troparion.text: At the First Council you appeared as a champion of Truth and a Wonderworker, O our God-bearing Father Spyridon.  / For you spoke to one dead in her grave, and transformed a serpent into gold;  / while chanting your holy prayers /  you had Angels serving with you, most holy one.  / Glory to Him Who glorified you.  / Glory to Him Who crowned you.  / Glory to Him Who, through you, grants healing to all.

kontakion.text: Wounded by the love of Christ, most holy one, your mind was given wings by the radiance of the Spirit, O God-inspired one,  / you found your work in active contemplation, O God-pleasing Spyridon,  / becoming a godly sacrifice, and entreating divine illumination for all.

troparion.text: By a flood of tears you made the desert fertile, / and your longing for God brought forth fruits in abundance. / By the radiance of miracles you illumined the whole universe! / O our holy father Therapon, pray to Christ our God to save our souls!

By sharing in the ways of the Apostles, / you became a successor to their throne. / Through the practice of virtue, you found the way to divine contemplation, O inspired one of God; / by teaching the word of truth without error, you defended the Faith, / even to the shedding of your blood. / Hieromartyr Alexander, entreat Christ God to save our souls.

Your holy martyr Synetus, O Lord, / through his sufferings has received an incorruptible crown from You, our God. / For having Your strength, he laid low his adversaries, / and shattered the powerless boldness of demons. / Through his intercessions, save our souls!

kontakion.text:
(Podoben: “You sought the heights...”)
You appeared as a bright star announcing Christ with your radiance,  / which is repulsive to this world, O Martyr Synetus;  / extinguishing the allure of false gods,  / you enlighten the faithful, /  always interceding for us all.

oca_saints.name: Martyr John, Abbot of the Zedazeni Monastery

troparion.text: By sharing in the ways of the Apostles, /  you became a successor to their throne. / Through the practice of virtue, / you found the way to divine contemplation, O inspired one of God; / by teaching the word of truth without error, / you defended the Faith, even to the shedding of your blood. / Hieromartyr John entreat Christ God to save our souls.
